title: Parallelize independent sub-reconcilers
description:
# GitHub Issue #188: Parallelize independent sub-reconcilers for reduced reconcile latency

**Category**: performance | **Scope**: Large

**Description**: Refactor the reconcile loop in keystone_controller.go to identify and run independent sub-reconcilers concurrently using errgroup. After reconcileConfig (which produces configMapName needed by downstream reconcilers), run reconcileFernetKeys, reconcileCredentialKeys, and reconcileNetworkPolicy in parallel since they have no data dependency on each other. Merge results and continue with dependent reconcilers sequentially.

**Motivation**: The 10 sub-reconcilers currently execute fully sequentially (keystone_controller.go:84-131). Some are provably independent and could run concurrently, reducing end-to-end reconcile latency. This matters most during initial CR creation when all sub-resources must be created, and during upgrades when multiple phases execute.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Independent sub-reconcilers run concurrently via errgroup
- [ ] Dependency graph between sub-reconcilers is documented
- [ ] Error from any parallel sub-reconciler cancels the group and is returned
- [ ] Condition updates from parallel sub-reconcilers do not race (use mutex or sequential condition writes)
- [ ] Reconcile latency reduced by at least 30% for initial CR creation in benchmarks
- [ ] No behavioral change in reconcile outcomes compared to sequential execution

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/188

stories.title: Operator runs independent sub-reconcilers in parallel for faster reconciliation
stories.want: the Keystone controller to reconcile independent sub-resources concurrently via errgroup
stories.so_that: initial CR creation and upgrades complete faster with reduced end-to-end reconcile latency
criteria: reconcileFernetKeys, reconcileCredentialKeys, and reconcileNetworkPolicy run concurrently via errgroup.WithContext after reconcileConfig completes
All three parallel sub-reconcilers set their respective conditions (FernetKeysReady, CredentialKeysReady, NetworkPolicyReady) correctly on success
Sequential sub-reconcilers (Secrets, Config, Database, Deployment, HPA, Bootstrap, TrustFlush) continue to execute in their current order
The parallel group executes after reconcileConfig and before reconcileDatabase

stories.title: Operator propagates errors from parallel sub-reconcilers correctly
stories.want: errors from any parallel sub-reconciler to cancel the errgroup context and surface as the Reconcile error
stories.so_that: I can diagnose failures without ambiguity about which sub-reconciler failed
criteria: An error from any parallel sub-reconciler cancels the errgroup context via errgroup.WithContext
The first error from the parallel group is returned as the Reconcile error
Conditions set by sub-reconcilers that completed before the error are preserved in status
The error triggers controller-runtime exponential backoff via standard error return

stories.title: Operator maintains thread-safe condition updates during parallel execution
stories.want: parallel sub-reconcilers to update status conditions without data races
stories.so_that: the operator passes go test -race and condition data is never corrupted
criteria: Each parallel sub-reconciler operates on its own DeepCopy of the Keystone object
Conditions from parallel copies are merged into the original sequentially after errgroup.Wait returns
No concurrent writes to the shared Status.Conditions slice occur
go test -race passes for all controller unit and integration tests

stories.title: Developer understands the sub-reconciler dependency graph from documentation
stories.want: the dependency graph between all sub-reconcilers to be documented in the reconciler reference
stories.so_that: I can safely identify future parallelization opportunities and understand execution ordering
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md contains a dependency graph table listing each sub-reconciler with its inputs, outputs, and condition type
The Reconciliation Flow ASCII diagram is updated to show the parallel group
The Sequential Execution Contract section is updated to reflect partial parallelization
The deep-copy condition merging pattern and errgroup usage are documented

stories.title: Operator achieves measurable latency reduction for initial CR creation
stories.want: the reconcile loop to complete at least 30% faster for initial CR creation
stories.so_that: the Keystone Identity Service is available sooner after CR creation
criteria: A BenchmarkReconcile test exists that measures end-to-end reconcile latency with a fake client
The benchmark uses all required sub-resources pre-created (DB sync job, bootstrap job, deployment, secrets)
Benchmark results demonstrate measurable latency reduction from parallel execution
No behavioral change in reconcile outcomes (same conditions, same resources created) compared to sequential execution

requirements.description: The reconcile loop SHALL run reconcileFernetKeys, reconcileCredentialKeys, and reconcileNetworkPolicy concurrently via errgroup.WithContext after reconcileConfig completes and before reconcileDatabase starts
requirements.rationale: These three sub-reconcilers have no data dependency on each other. FernetKeys and CredentialKeys depend on configMapName (from reconcileConfig) but not on each other. NetworkPolicy depends only on the CR spec. Running them concurrently reduces the critical path from sum(latencies) to max(latencies).

scenarios.name: All three parallel reconcilers succeed
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig completes successfully and all three parallel sub-reconcilers succeed
scenarios.then: FernetKeysReady, CredentialKeysReady, and NetworkPolicyReady conditions are all set to True on the Keystone status
and_then: reconcileDatabase starts after the parallel group completes
the Reconcile method continues with sequential sub-reconcilers

scenarios.name: Parallel group receives configMapName from reconcileConfig
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig returns configMapName without error
scenarios.then: the parallel group starts with three goroutines: FernetKeys and CredentialKeys receive configMapName, NetworkPolicy receives only the Keystone CR
and_then: all three goroutines run within the same errgroup

scenarios.name: Sequential sub-reconcilers maintain original ordering
scenarios.when: the parallel group completes without error or requeue
scenarios.then: Database, Deployment, HPA, Bootstrap, and TrustFlush execute sequentially in that order
and_then: each sequential sub-reconciler still early-returns on error or non-zero RequeueAfter

requirements.description: An error from any parallel sub-reconciler SHALL cancel the errgroup context and the first error SHALL be returned as the Reconcile error
requirements.rationale: errgroup.WithContext cancels the derived context when any goroutine returns a non-nil error. This matches the current sequential behavior where an error in one sub-reconciler prevents subsequent ones from proceeding. Remaining goroutines receive context.Canceled on their next API call.

scenarios.name: Single parallel reconciler errors
scenarios.when: one of the three parallel sub-reconcilers returns a non-nil error (e.g. reconcileFernetKeys fails to create a Secret)
scenarios.then: the errgroup context is cancelled and g.Wait() returns the error
and_then: the Reconcile method returns the error via updateStatus
controller-runtime applies exponential backoff

scenarios.name: Conditions from successful parallel reconcilers preserved on error
scenarios.when: reconcileFernetKeys errors but reconcileNetworkPolicy already completed successfully
scenarios.then: NetworkPolicyReady condition is merged from its DeepCopy into the original Keystone status
and_then: updateStatus persists both the error-related state and the successful condition

scenarios.name: Context cancellation propagates to in-flight goroutines
scenarios.when: the errgroup context is cancelled because one goroutine returned an error
scenarios.then: remaining goroutines' Kubernetes API calls (r.Get, r.Create, r.Update) receive context.Canceled and return promptly
and_then: errgroup.Wait returns after all goroutines have exited

requirements.description: Condition updates from parallel sub-reconcilers SHALL NOT race on the shared Status.Conditions slice; each parallel goroutine SHALL operate on a DeepCopy and conditions SHALL be merged sequentially after errgroup.Wait returns
requirements.rationale: meta.SetStatusCondition modifies a shared []metav1.Condition slice via linear scan and in-place update/append. It is not thread-safe. The DeepCopy-per-goroutine pattern eliminates shared mutable state entirely, making races impossible by construction.

scenarios.name: No concurrent writes via deep copy isolation
scenarios.when: three parallel sub-reconcilers run concurrently via errgroup
scenarios.then: each goroutine receives its own DeepCopy of the Keystone object and calls conditions.SetCondition only on its copy
and_then: no two goroutines write to the same []metav1.Condition slice

scenarios.name: Race detector passes for all controller tests
scenarios.when: go test -race ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... is executed
scenarios.then: no data races are reported for the parallel execution path or any other test

scenarios.name: Condition merge preserves pre-existing conditions
scenarios.when: conditions from three parallel copies are merged into the original after g.Wait()
scenarios.then: only the specific condition type for each parallel reconciler (FernetKeysReady, CredentialKeysReady, NetworkPolicyReady) is extracted and set on the original
and_then: conditions from prior sequential reconcilers (SecretsReady) remain unchanged on the original

requirements.description: If any parallel sub-reconciler returns a non-zero ctrl.Result (RequeueAfter or Requeue), the parallel group SHALL return the shortest non-zero RequeueAfter
requirements.rationale: When multiple parallel reconcilers request requeues with different intervals, the shortest interval ensures the earliest pending work is re-evaluated first. This preserves the semantic that reconciliation retries as early as needed.

scenarios.name: Single requeue from parallel group
scenarios.when: one parallel sub-reconciler returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 15s} and the other two return ctrl.Result{}
scenarios.then: the parallel group result is ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 15s}
and_then: Reconcile early-returns with this result via updateStatus

scenarios.name: Multiple requeues pick shortest interval
scenarios.when: two parallel sub-reconcilers return non-zero RequeueAfter (e.g. 15s and 30s)
scenarios.then: shortestRequeue returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 15s} (the minimum)

scenarios.name: No requeues returns zero result
scenarios.when: all three parallel sub-reconcilers return ctrl.Result{} (zero value)
scenarios.then: shortestRequeue returns ctrl.Result{} (zero value)
and_then: Reconcile continues with sequential sub-reconcilers

requirements.description: Sequential sub-reconcilers SHALL continue to execute in the order: Secrets → Config → [parallel group] → Database → Deployment → HPA → Bootstrap → TrustFlush → setReadyCondition
requirements.rationale: The remaining sub-reconcilers have data dependencies requiring sequential execution: Database needs configMapName, Deployment needs configMapName and implicitly depends on Database being ready, Bootstrap needs a running Deployment, TrustFlush needs configMapName.

scenarios.name: Database runs after parallel group completes
scenarios.when: the parallel group completes successfully with no requeue
scenarios.then: reconcileDatabase is called with the configMapName from reconcileConfig
and_then: reconcileDeployment follows reconcileDatabase if Database succeeds

scenarios.name: Early return on sequential sub-reconciler error preserved
scenarios.when: reconcileDatabase returns an error after the parallel group succeeded
scenarios.then: reconcileDeployment, HPA, Bootstrap, and TrustFlush are skipped
and_then: the error is returned via updateStatus with all conditions set so far

scenarios.name: Early return on sequential sub-reconciler requeue preserved
scenarios.when: reconcileDatabase returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: 30s} (database not ready)
scenarios.then: reconcileDeployment and all subsequent reconcilers are skipped
and_then: the requeue result is returned via updateStatus

requirements.description: The dependency graph between all sub-reconcilers SHALL be documented in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md with inputs, outputs, and parallelization status for each
requirements.rationale: An explicit dependency graph enables developers to identify future parallelization opportunities, understand why certain sub-reconcilers must remain sequential, and validate that the parallel group is correct.

scenarios.name: Dependency graph table documents all sub-reconcilers
scenarios.when: a developer opens docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md
scenarios.then: a table lists each sub-reconciler with columns: Name, Inputs, Outputs, Condition Type, Parallel-eligible
and_then: the parallel group members are clearly identified

scenarios.name: Flow diagram shows parallel execution
scenarios.when: a developer reads the Reconciliation Flow section
scenarios.then: the ASCII diagram shows FernetKeys, CredentialKeys, and NetworkPolicy in a parallel block with errgroup notation
and_then: sequential sub-reconcilers before and after the parallel block are shown with arrows

scenarios.name: Deep-copy condition merge pattern documented
scenarios.when: a developer reads the updated documentation
scenarios.then: the rationale for using DeepCopy per goroutine and sequential condition merging is explained
and_then: the alternative (mutex) approach is mentioned with rationale for why DeepCopy was chosen

requirements.description: Reconcile latency SHOULD be reduced by at least 30% for initial CR creation as measured by BenchmarkReconcile
requirements.rationale: Running 3 independent sub-reconcilers in parallel reduces the critical path from sum(FernetKeys + CredentialKeys + NetworkPolicy) to max(FernetKeys, CredentialKeys, NetworkPolicy). With a fake client the improvement comes from reduced goroutine scheduling overhead; with a real API server the improvement is proportional to I/O parallelism.

scenarios.name: Benchmark measures full reconcile cycle
scenarios.when: BenchmarkReconcile runs with a fake client and all required sub-resources pre-created
scenarios.then: the benchmark reports ns/op for the complete Reconcile call including all sub-reconcilers
and_then: the benchmark is deterministic and reproducible in CI

scenarios.name: Parallel execution measurably reduces latency
scenarios.when: benchmark results are compared against a sequential baseline (running the same sub-reconcilers sequentially)
scenarios.then: the parallel version demonstrates reduced latency for the parallel group phase

requirements.description: There SHALL be no behavioral change in reconcile outcomes compared to sequential execution: same resources created, same conditions set, same errors returned
requirements.rationale: The parallelization is a pure performance optimization. All existing unit tests and integration tests must pass without modification, confirming behavioral equivalence.

scenarios.name: All existing unit tests pass unchanged
scenarios.when: all existing tests in keystone_controller_test.go are executed
scenarios.then: all tests pass without modification including TestReconcile_SetsAllSubConditionsTrue, TestReconcile_AggregatesReadyCondition, TestReconcile_Idempotent

scenarios.name: All existing integration tests pass unchanged
scenarios.when: integration_test.go tests are executed with the -race flag
scenarios.then: all integration tests pass without modification
and_then: no data races detected by the race detector

scenarios.name: Reconcile idempotency preserved
scenarios.when: Reconcile is called twice on the same Keystone CR with all sub-resources ready
scenarios.then: the second call produces the same ctrl.Result and conditions as the first
and_then: all conditions remain stable across reconcile cycles

tasks.title: Add shortestRequeue and mergeParallelConditions helper functions to keystone_controller.go with unit tests (CC-0071, REQ-003, REQ-004)
tasks.description: Add two helper functions to operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go: (1) shortestRequeue(results ...ctrl.Result) ctrl.Result that returns the minimum non-zero RequeueAfter from the given results, returning ctrl.Result{} if all are zero. (2) mergeParallelConditions(dst *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone, sources ...parallelConditionSource) that extracts a specific condition type from each source's DeepCopy and sets it on the destination using conditions.SetCondition. Define parallelConditionSource struct {conditionType string; source *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone}. Add unit tests in keystone_controller_test.go: TestShortestRequeue_AllZero, TestShortestRequeue_SingleNonZero, TestShortestRequeue_PicksMinimum, TestMergeParallelConditions_MergesCorrectly, TestMergeParallelConditions_SkipsMissingCondition, TestMergeParallelConditions_PreservesExistingConditions.

tasks.title: Implement reconcileParallelGroup method with errgroup.WithContext and per-goroutine DeepCopy in keystone_controller.go (CC-0071, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Add a new method reconcileParallelGroup(ctx, keystone, configMapName) (ctrl.Result, error) to KeystoneReconciler in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go. Implementation: (1) Create 3 DeepCopy instances of keystone. (2) Create errgroup.WithContext(ctx). (3) Launch 3 goroutines: reconcileFernetKeys(gctx, ksCopyFernet, configMapName), reconcileCredentialKeys(gctx, ksCopyCred, configMapName), reconcileNetworkPolicy(gctx, ksCopyNetpol). Capture ctrl.Result from each in closure variables. (4) Call g.Wait() to collect the first error. (5) Call mergeParallelConditions to extract FernetKeysReady, CredentialKeysReady, NetworkPolicyReady from copies into original. (6) If error, return it. (7) Return shortestRequeue of the three results. Restructure Reconcile to call reconcileParallelGroup between reconcileConfig and reconcileDatabase with the standard early-return pattern. Import golang.org/x/sync/errgroup, run go mod tidy to promote it from indirect to direct dependency.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for parallel Reconcile: success path, error cancellation, and partial condition merge (CC-0071, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-008)
tasks.description: Add tests in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go: (1) TestReconcile_ParallelGroupSetsAllConditions: full reconcile with all sub-resources ready, verify FernetKeysReady, CredentialKeysReady, and NetworkPolicyReady are all True (validates parallel group produces same outcome as sequential). (2) TestReconcileParallelGroup_ErrorCancelsAndReturns: use fake client with interceptor.Funcs to inject an error on the Create call for the fernet keys Secret, verify Reconcile returns a non-nil error, verify conditions from successful parallel reconcilers (e.g. NetworkPolicyReady) are still merged. (3) TestReconcileParallelGroup_RequeueMerged: mock a sub-reconciler that returns RequeueAfter, verify reconcileParallelGroup returns the requeue result. Use existing test helpers (testKeystone, testReadyExternalSecrets, newTestReconciler, testComputeConfigMapName).

tasks.title: Add BenchmarkReconcile measuring end-to-end reconcile latency with fake client (CC-0071, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Add BenchmarkReconcile_FullReconcile in operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go. Setup: create a fake client with all required objects pre-loaded (testKeystone, testReadyExternalSecrets, testDBCredentialsSecret, testAdminCredentialsSecret, testCompletedDBSyncJob, testCompletedSchemaCheckJob, testCompletedBootstrapJob, testReadyKeystoneDeployment, testFernetKeysSecret, testCredentialKeysSecret). In b.ResetTimer/b.Run loop, call r.Reconcile(ctx, req) and verify no error. This measures the full reconcile cycle including the parallel group. Document the baseline ns/op result in the PR description for comparison.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md with parallel flow diagram, dependency graph, and condition merge pattern (CC-0071, REQ-006)
tasks.description: Update operators/keystone/docs (actually docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md): (1) Replace the sequential Reconciliation Flow ASCII diagram with a new diagram showing the parallel group (FernetKeys, CredentialKeys, NetworkPolicy in a box with 'errgroup.WithContext' label) between Config and Database. (2) Add a Sub-Reconciler Dependency Graph table with columns: Sub-Reconciler, Inputs, Outputs/Condition, Dependencies, Parallel-eligible. (3) Update the Sequential Execution Contract section to describe the hybrid sequential+parallel pattern. (4) Add a Parallel Execution section documenting: the DeepCopy-per-goroutine pattern, mergeParallelConditions, shortestRequeue, and why mutex was not chosen (DeepCopy eliminates shared state entirely). (5) Add CC-0071 to the feature list in the frontmatter.

test_specifications.expected: Existing test: Ready condition aggregation works correctly with parallel-set sub-conditions [unit]

review_criteria: All existing unit tests in keystone_controller_test.go pass without modification (TestReconcile_SetsAllSubConditionsTrue, TestReconcile_AggregatesReadyCondition, TestReconcile_Idempotent, TestReconcile_EarlyReturnOnSecretsNotReady)
All existing integration tests in integration_test.go pass without modification under go test -race
New unit tests cover: parallel success (all conditions True), parallel error (cancellation + partial merge), shortestRequeue (all scenarios), mergeParallelConditions (merge, skip, preserve)
go test -race -count=1 ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/... reports zero data races
Only keystone_controller.go, keystone_controller_test.go, docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md, and go.mod/go.sum are modified — no changes to sub-reconciler implementation files

golangci-lint run ./operators/keystone/... passes with no errors
implementation_notes:
## Architectural Decision: DeepCopy per goroutine vs Mutex

The core design decision is how to prevent data races on keystone.Status.Conditions when parallel sub-reconcilers call conditions.SetCondition concurrently.

**Chosen: DeepCopy per goroutine with sequential merge.** Each parallel goroutine receives its own DeepCopy of the Keystone object. After errgroup.Wait(), conditions are extracted from each copy and set on the original sequentially. This eliminates shared mutable state by construction.

**Alternative considered: sync.Mutex on condition writes.** Would require adding a conditionMu field to KeystoneReconciler and modifying all 3 parallel sub-reconcilers to use a locked SetCondition variant. Rejected because it increases blast radius (3 additional files changed) and introduces a new synchronization primitive that could be misused.

## Architecture Diagram

```
Reconcile(ctx, req)
├── r.Get(Keystone CR)                    [sequential]
├── reconcileSecrets(ctx, &ks)             [sequential] → SecretsReady
├── reconcileConfig(ctx, &ks)              [sequential] → configMapName
├── reconcileParallelGroup(ctx, &ks, cfgMap)  [NEW - CC-0071]
│   ├── DeepCopy × 3
│   ├── errgroup.WithContext(ctx)
│   │   ├── go: reconcileFernetKeys(gctx, copy1, cfgMap)    → FernetKeysReady
│   │   ├── go: reconcileCredentialKeys(gctx, copy2, cfgMap) → CredentialKeysReady
│   │   └── go: reconcileNetworkPolicy(gctx, copy3)          → NetworkPolicyReady
│   ├── g.Wait()
│   ├── mergeParallelConditions(ks, copy1, copy2, copy3)
│   └── shortestRequeue(result1, result2, result3)
├── reconcileDatabase(ctx, &ks, cfgMap)    [sequential] → DatabaseReady
├── reconcileDeployment(ctx, &ks, cfgMap)  [sequential] → DeploymentReady
├── reconcileHPA(ctx, &ks)                 [sequential] → HPAReady
├── reconcileBootstrap(ctx, &ks, cfgMap)   [sequential] → BootstrapReady
├── reconcileTrustFlush(ctx, &ks, cfgMap)  [sequential] → TrustFlushReady
├── setReadyCondition(&ks)                 [sequential] → Ready
└── updateStatus(ctx, &ks, result, err)    [sequential]
```

## Data Flow for Parallel Group

```
Keystone CR (original, with SecretsReady set)
    │
    ├─ DeepCopy → ksCopyFernet ──[goroutine 1]──→ FernetKeysReady on copy
    ├─ DeepCopy → ksCopyCred   ──[goroutine 2]──→ CredentialKeysReady on copy
    └─ DeepCopy → ksCopyNetpol ──[goroutine 3]──→ NetworkPolicyReady on copy
                                                     │
                                          errgroup.Wait()
                                                     │
                                    mergeParallelConditions:
                                      copy1 → FernetKeysReady → original
                                      copy2 → CredentialKeysReady → original
                                      copy3 → NetworkPolicyReady → original
```

## Sub-Reconciler Dependency Graph

| Sub-Reconciler | Inputs | Condition | Dependencies | Parallel |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| reconcileSecrets | CR spec | SecretsReady | none | no (must be first) |
| reconcileConfig | CR spec, DB secret | (returns configMapName) | Secrets | no (produces configMapName) |
| reconcileFernetKeys | configMapName | FernetKeysReady | Config | **yes** |
| reconcileCredentialKeys | configMapName | CredentialKeysReady | Config | **yes** |
| reconcileNetworkPolicy | CR spec | NetworkPolicyReady | none | **yes** |
| reconcileDatabase | configMapName | DatabaseReady | Config | no (complex state machine) |
| reconcileDeployment | configMapName | DeploymentReady | Config, Database (implicit) | no |
| reconcileHPA | CR spec | HPAReady | Deployment (naming) | no |
| reconcileBootstrap | configMapName | BootstrapReady | Deployment (API must be running) | no |
| reconcileTrustFlush | configMapName | TrustFlushReady | Config | no |

## Potential Pitfalls

1. **Do NOT pass the original keystone pointer to goroutines** — conditions.SetCondition on the shared slice will race. Always use DeepCopy.
2. **Always merge conditions after g.Wait()** — even on error, partial conditions from successful goroutines should be merged so the status reflects actual state.
3. **Use errgroup.WithContext, not plain errgroup.Group** — context cancellation is needed so remaining goroutines exit promptly on first error.
4. **The result variables (fernetResult, credResult, netpolResult) are safe to read after g.Wait()** — errgroup.Wait provides the happens-before guarantee.
5. **Do NOT include reconcileDatabase in the parallel group** — it has a complex state machine with requeue logic (30s waits for MariaDB CRs, db_sync Job, schema-check Job) and reconcileDeployment depends on it implicitly.
